generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatBedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heating element modulating internal temperature through resistive heating. Controlled via Raspberry Pi using bang-bang strategy (on/off switching) based on internal temperature sensor feedback. No explicit PWM or advanced control protocols mentioned."
]

instance IncubatorFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Distribution mechanism ensuring uniform thermal distribution inside the styrofoam box. Controlled via Raspberry Pi alongside the heatbed."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Central embedded controller interfacing with actuators (heatbed/fan) and sensors via direct digital outputs. Implements bang-bang control strategy without remote control interfaces."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring internal temperature of the incubator box. Temperature range: -55°C to +125°C, accuracy: ±0.5°C. Operates on 1-Wire bus."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring internal temperature of the incubator box. Temperature range: -55°C to +125°C, accuracy: ±0.5°C. Operates on 1-Wire bus."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer monitoring ambient external temperature. Temperature range: -55°C to +125°C, accuracy: ±0.5°C. Operates on 1-Wire bus."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataStream1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
    base:desc "Periodic transmission of internal temperature sensor data from Physical Twin to Digital Twin via RabbitMQ message broker. Implied synchronization with controller's control loop."
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataStream2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Periodic transmission of internal temperature sensor data from Physical Twin to Digital Twin via RabbitMQ message broker. Implied synchronization with controller's control loop."
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Periodic transmission of ambient temperature sensor data from Physical Twin to Digital Twin via RabbitMQ message broker. Implied synchronization with controller's control loop."
]

instance SensorDataAggregation : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureDataStream1, InternalTemperatureDataStream2, ExternalTemperatureDataStream
    base:desc "Low-level driver aggregates and transmits combined sensor data to RabbitMQ server for Digital Twin consumption."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Differential equation-based behavioral model with two free parameters representing thermal dynamics of the incubator. Used for predictive simulations with lower fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, MonitoringAlgorithm
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Differential equation-based behavioral model with four free parameters capturing higher-fidelity thermal inertia of the heatbed. Preferred for predictive accuracy and fault detection."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, MonitoringAlgorithm, PredictiveMaintenanceAlgorithm
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous time-series temperature measurements from three sensors (two internal, one external) collected for real-time monitoring and control purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataAggregation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, MonitoringAlgorithm
]

instance CalibrationParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia) updated via iterative optimization techniques to minimize prediction errors between Physical and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FourParameterThermalModel, TwoParameterThermalModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight open-source message broker facilitating asynchronous communication between Physical Twin components and Digital Twin. Supports periodic sensor data transmission."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataIngestionService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance ScipyNumericalEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based numerical library for solving differential equations and implementing calibration algorithms (e.g., least squares, Bayesian inference)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, SimulationService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Iterative optimization technique dynamically updating model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia) using real-time sensor data to minimize prediction errors."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance MonitoringAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Fault detection and state estimation algorithms assessing system performance against safety and operational thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Long-term data analysis algorithm predicting component degradation (e.g., heatbed efficiency loss) and thermal runaway risks."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous ingestion of temperature sensor data (three sensors) to track thermal state of the system in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalStateInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven calibration of simulation models using algorithms that update model parameters based on operational discrepancies between Physical and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterUpdateAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Reproducible simulations of Physical Twin's behavior under current operating conditions enabling what-if analysis (e.g., environmental changes, internal load variations)."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveSimulationInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time assessment of system performance against safety and operational thresholds using fault detection and state estimation algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemPerformanceInsight, SafetyViolationAlert
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Long-term analysis predicting component degradation (e.g., heatbed efficiency loss) and identifying potential failure modes."
    DTDFVocab:provides DegradationPredictionInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of model parameters and control signals to minimize prediction errors and maintain optimal system performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Advanced visualizations of Physical Twin's state (e.g., temperature gradients, control signals) for debugging, validation, and user interaction."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualDiagnosticInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ThermalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of internal and external temperature measurements to understand current thermal state of the incubator."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of model prediction accuracy compared to Physical Twin observations, highlighting operational discrepancies."
]

instance PredictiveSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated temperature dynamics under hypothetical scenarios (e.g., environmental changes) to evaluate system robustness."
]

instance SystemPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of system performance against predefined safety and operational thresholds."
]

instance SafetyViolationAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic alert triggered when system performance deviates from safety thresholds (e.g., overheating risks)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia) based on calibration algorithm results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to control parameters (e.g., heatbed power, fan speed) to optimize system performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DegradationPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted component degradation trends (e.g., heatbed efficiency loss) based on long-term data analysis."
]

instance VisualDiagnosticInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical representation of temperature gradients, control signals, and system state for diagnostic purposes."
]

// C1: Environment
instance IncubatorPhysicalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical space containing the styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, and temperature sensors."
    base:contains HeatBedActuator, IncubatorFanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinCyberEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Cyber space hosting simulation models, calibration algorithms, and monitoring services."
    base:contains TwoParameterThermalModel, FourParameterThermalModel, CalibrationAlgorithm, MonitoringAlgorithm, PredictiveMaintenanceAlgorithm
]

instance ControlCyberEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Embedded control system executing real-time control logic and interfacing with Physical Twin."
    base:contains RaspberryPiController
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the incubator DT system involves **control commands** generated by the Digital Twin (or its embedded controller) and transmitted to the Physical Twin (PT) via the **RabbitMQ message broker**. The controller implements a **modified bang-bang control strategy**, where commands are issued to the **heatbed** (for heating) and **fan** (for air distribution) based on temperature measurements. The **command types** include:
  - **Heating activation** (heatbed ON/OFF) with a configurable **heating duration (H)**.
  - **Waiting duration (C)** after heating to prevent overshoot.
  - **Temperature thresholds** defined by **lower limit (LL)** and **upper limit (UL)**.
The **control message format** is not explicitly detailed but is inferred to be structured as **asynchronous messages** published to RabbitMQ queues, consumed by the **low-level driver**. No explicit **validation/authentication mechanisms** are mentioned, though the **safe protocol** in the low-level driver implies basic safety checks (e.g., preventing invalid commands). **Feedback systems** are implicit via the **closed-loop control**: the PT’s sensor data is continuously fed back to the DT for recalibration and monitoring. **Conflict resolution** is not addressed, but the **statechart-based controller** (Figure 8) suggests deterministic command execution. **Command execution confirmation** is not explicitly described, though the low-level driver’s role in issuing PT states to RabbitMQ implies acknowledgment of command receipt."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator employs a **periodic data acquisition** mechanism where the low-level driver reads temperature data from three sensors (two internal and one external) and relays it to the controller via RabbitMQ. While the exact sampling frequency is not specified in the documents, the system is designed to support **real-time visualization** of the incubator status by storing data in a time-series database and plotting it into a dashboard. The temporal granularity is implied to be fine enough to capture dynamic thermal behavior, including the **thermal inertia of the heatbed**, which requires accurate modeling of transient responses. The system also supports **on-demand calibration** and **anomaly detection** via a Kalman filter, suggesting that temporal synchronization between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) is critical for maintaining model accuracy. Latency requirements are not explicitly quantified, but the use of a Kalman filter and co-simulations for *what-if* analysis implies low-latency data processing to enable real-time decision-making and controller reconfiguration."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator follows a **three-layer architecture** comprising **Data**, **Models**, and **Algorithms**, as outlined in Document 1. The **Data layer** is sourced from **three temperature sensors** (two internal, one external) embedded in the Physical Twin (PT), with data relayed via **RabbitMQ** to the Raspberry Pi. The **Models layer** includes **two calibrated simulation models** (Model A and Model B), where Model B is preferred for its accuracy in predicting heatbed dynamics. The **Algorithms layer** involves **calibration techniques** (as described in Section 3.3) to refine model parameters based on real-time sensor data. The system lacks explicit mention of **orchestration patterns**, **resource management**, or **system-wide coordination mechanisms** beyond the centralized Raspberry Pi controller. The schematic in Document 1 (Figure 1) illustrates a **closed-loop structure** where calibration algorithms update models, monitors assess system performance, and human/algorithm-driven interventions adjust the PT as needed."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the incubator case study includes the following phases: **(1) Development Phase**: Creation of abstract representations (models) of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, such as thermal models with varying levels of abstraction (e.g., a model with **two free parameters** and another with **four free parameters** for predicting temperature dynamics). **(2) Integration Phase**: Models are integrated into the DT framework alongside **real-time sensor data** (e.g., temperature sensors inside/outside the insulated container) and **calibration algorithms** to refine model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia of the heatbed). **(3) Deployment Phase**: The DT is deployed to monitor the Physical Twin (PT) in real-time, using **monitoring algorithms** to assess safety and optimality. **(4) Evolution Phase**: Continuous updates via **calibration algorithms** and **feedback loops** (e.g., adjusting models based on deviations detected by monitors). Transition management involves **version control** of models (e.g., switching between simpler/faster models and more accurate but computationally intensive ones) and **governance** through human/algorithm-driven interventions when violations (e.g., temperature deviations) are detected. The lifecycle emphasizes **retention of models post-deployment** to enable predictive simulations and adaptive control."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The incubator DT’s **fidelity and validity** are governed by:
1. **Model fidelity requirements**:
   - **Trade-off between accuracy and computational efficiency**: The **two-parameter model** is simpler but less precise, while the **four-parameter model** captures **thermal inertia** (e.g., heatbed mass effects) with higher fidelity. The latter is preferred for **predictive simulations** but may require **more intensive calibration**.
   - **Abstraction levels**: Models are **behavioral** (differential equations) rather than **geometric** (e.g., CAD-based), focusing on **dynamic thermal responses** rather than static structural properties.
2. **Validation and verification methods**:
   - **Experimental validation**: Real-world data from the PT is used to **compare predicted vs. observed temperatures**, with the **four-parameter model** showing superior performance in capturing **transient thermal behavior** (e.g., response to step changes in setpoint).
   - **Calibration techniques**: **Iterative optimization** (e.g., least-squares fitting) adjusts model parameters to minimize errors between simulated and measured temperatures. The process is **automated** within the DT’s algorithmic framework.
3. **Uncertainty sources and quantification**:
   - **Sensor noise**: Internal/external temperature measurements may introduce **±0.1°C uncertainty** (typical for digital sensors), propagated into model predictions.
   - **Environmental variability**: Unmodeled factors (e.g., door openings, humidity) act as **external disturbances**, quantified via **residual analysis** in the calibration step.
   - **Model simplification errors**: The **four-parameter model** assumes lumped thermal capacitance; deviations from this ideal (e.g., spatial temperature gradients) are **uncertainty sources** addressed via **sensor redundancy**.
4. **Error detection and correction mechanisms**:
   - **Monitoring algorithms**: Continuously compare **PT sensor data** with **DT predictions**; discrepancies beyond thresholds (e.g., **±1°C**) trigger:
     - **Recalibration** of model parameters.
     - **Fault isolation** (e.g., identifying a failed sensor or actuator).
   - **Safety nets**: If errors persist, the system defaults to **conservative control** (e.g., reducing heatbed power) or **human override**.
5. **Quality assurance processes**:
   - **Open-source reproducibility**: The **public repository** enables third-party validation of models and data pipelines, ensuring transparency.
   - **Modular design**: Allows **swapping components** (e.g., sensors, models) without disrupting the entire system, facilitating **continuous improvement**.
   - **Documented metrics**: Experimental results (Section 4) quantify **model accuracy** (e.g., RMSE between predictions and measurements), serving as benchmarks for future iterations.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical connection** for the incubator DT system relies on a **RabbitMQ message broker**, deployed as a **lightweight, open-source message broker** supporting **asynchronous communication** between components. The network architecture follows a **centralized hub-and-spoke model**, where the RabbitMQ server acts as the intermediary for all messages exchanged between the **low-level driver (PT interface)**, **controller (Raspberry Pi)**, and **DT components**. The system does not specify explicit **bandwidth/latency requirements**, but the use of RabbitMQ implies **low-latency, high-reliability communication** suitable for real-time control. **Security protocols** are not detailed, though the **safe protocol** in the low-level driver ensures safe state transitions. The infrastructure is **on-premise**, with RabbitMQ and the controller running on the **same Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, eliminating external network dependencies. No redundancy or failover mechanisms are described."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment** of the incubator DT system is **fully on-premise**, with all critical components (RabbitMQ server, controller, and low-level driver) running on a **single Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**. The system does not utilize **cloud or edge deployment models**, nor does it describe **scalability mechanisms** beyond the local hardware constraints. The **computational requirements** are minimal, focusing on real-time temperature monitoring and control logic execution. **Storage requirements** are not explicitly quantified, but the system relies on **in-memory message queues** (RabbitMQ) for transient data. **Availability/reliability** is ensured by the **localized deployment**, though no explicit redundancy or backup mechanisms are mentioned. The DT components are **co-located** with the PT, enabling **low-latency interactions** without external dependencies."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its integration of **monitoring and control mechanisms** to ensure the physical twin (PT) operates within safe operational bounds. Specifically, the DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment, enabling real-time adjustments to maintain system performance. The system includes **monitors** that continuously check for deviations from safe and optimal conditions, leveraging data from sensors (e.g., two internal temperature sensors and one external sensor) to detect anomalies. Fail-safe mechanisms are implied through the **supervisory control loop**, where detected violations trigger human or algorithmic interventions to modify the physical twin’s behavior, such as adjusting the heatbed or fan operations. While explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., authentication, access control, or threat mitigation) are not detailed in the provided documents, the system’s reliance on **real-time data integrity** (via sensor inputs) and **model calibration** suggests inherent dependencies on secure data transmission and processing. The absence of explicit mention of compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001) or anonymization techniques indicates these aspects are not addressed in the current scope."
]
